title: (BUG) daemon/ws_events: broadcast channel lag drops events silently to slow SSE clients
description: In daemon/src/ws_events.rs, the WebSocket event loop handles RecvError::Lagged(n) (line 69) by logging a warning and continuing — it does not disconnect the slow client or send the client any signal that events were lost. The broadcast channel has capacity 256 (BROADCAST_CAPACITY in state.rs). A slow browser client that does not drain its WebSocket fast enough will silently miss up to n events. This is particularly problematic for message delivery events: the browser UI will appear to miss messages with no indication anything went wrong. Fix: on Lagged, send the client a synthetic 'events_dropped' JSON frame before continuing so the client knows it needs to refetch state, OR disconnect the client and let it reconnect and refetch.
